title: Gentianella pavonii
intro: Gentianella pavonii, also known as Pavon's gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, Gentianella pavonii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammatory diseases, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gentianella pavonii include gentiopicroside, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gentianella pavonii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
taxonomy: Gentianella pavonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Gentianella, which consists of small, delicate plants commonly known as gentians. Gentianella pavonii is native to South America and is a relatively rare species within its genus. The plant's classification is further defined by its binomial nomenclature, Gentianella pavonii, which is used to distinguish it from other closely related species. Gentianella pavonii is often found in high-altitude regions, where it grows in areas with rocky substrates and limited vegetation.

constituents: Gentianella pavonii contains iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside, which is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
